author: Dr. Priya Anand / Dr. Alan Whitfield
content:
MEDICINE CO-MANAGEMENT CONSULT — NEW ATRIAL FIBRILLATION
POD#1 s/p open cholecystectomy.

Reason for consult: Surgery requests Medicine co-management of new atrial fibrillation noted on telemetry.

OBJECTIVE DATA ASSEMBLED (this is a data-gathering note; management to be determined by the primary/covering team):
- Rhythm: brief, self-terminating runs of atrial fibrillation on telemetry (see telemetry event note NOTE004 for the detailed characterization — 3 runs, 15-20 seconds each, asymptomatic, self-limited). 12-lead ECGs preop (IMG002) and today (IMG003) both show normal sinus rhythm.
- Hemodynamics: normotensive, asymptomatic; no chest pain, dyspnea, palpitations, or lightheadedness.
- Clinical context: POD#1 after surgery for perforated cholecystitis. Electrolytes including potassium, magnesium, and calcium normal (LAB002). Troponin negative. SpO2 normal on room air. Pain controlled.
- Cardiac background: NO prior documented atrial fibrillation. NO prior echocardiogram on file. Outpatient cardiology (11/2026) found no structural heart disease on history/exam with a normal ECG, but no echo was performed.
- Anticoagulation status: patient is on NO therapeutic anticoagulant and NO antiplatelet at baseline; only prophylactic-dose enoxaparin (VTE prophylaxis) started today.
- Relevant comorbidities on the chart: poorly-controlled hypertension, type 2 diabetes, age 72, female. Recent major abdominal surgery (POD#1).

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
#New atrial fibrillation noted on telemetry. Hemodynamically stable, asymptomatic.
- Continuous telemetry in place.
(Further evaluation and management to be determined by the team.)
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Priya Anand, MD (PGY-2) and Dr. Alan Whitfield, MD (Medicine attending). 6/11/2027.
